Because shawls are traditionally meant to have a lovely drape and an open, airy lace look, designers heavily rely on **chains** and **double crochets**. Therefore, hdc stitches likely make up only about **5.8%** of the total stitches in the average shawl.

12,000,000 (shawls) × 38,000 (stitches per shawl) = 456,000,000,000 total stitches.
